title: 10 best herbal essential oils for angina
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for angina are plant-based remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of angina by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation. The best herbal essential oils for angina include Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Crataegus monogyna, Valeriana officinalis, Rauvolfia serpentina, Lavandula angustifolia, Commiphora molmol, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, angina can severely impact one's quality of life, causing recurring chest pain, breathlessness, and fatigue, ultimately limiting daily activities and hindering social interactions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with angina because they contain compounds that improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. When inhaled, the oils dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle, which can help alleviate chest pain associated with angina. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help protect the cardiovascular system from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing the risk of anginal episodes. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation, rosemary essential oils may provide relief from angina symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of angina by protecting against damage to blood vessels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina by improving blood flow and reducing the risk of cardiac events.

Cardiovascular vasodilatory properties: Rosemary essential oil has been shown to have a positive effect on blood vessel function, relaxing and dilating blood vessels to improve circulation and reduce blood pressure, which may help alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and improving blood flow to the heart.
Stems: Containing bioactive compounds that help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and mild soap for 15 minutes.
Steam distill 100g of the cleaned leaves in a still using 500ml of water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Combine the resulting essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 3% ratio and mix well.
Store the finished essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with angina because it has a calming effect on the cardiovascular system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate symptoms. The oil's natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing chest pain associated with angina. Additionally, its sedative qualities can help regulate heart rate and rhythm, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness, further alleviating anginal symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: English Lavender essential oil contains powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to anginal pain.
Vasodilatory: The oil's vasodilatory properties help to relax constricted blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest tightness and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiac tissue, which can exacerbate anginal pain and damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain bioactive compounds like camphor and borneol, which contribute to the oil's anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, making it effective in alleviating angina symptoms.

Seeds: English lavender seeds are a rich source of essential fatty acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, thereby providing relief from anginal episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using a warm air circulation oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until crispy brittle.
Crush the dried lavandula angustifolia leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for maximum oil release.
Mix 50 grams of the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days before straining through cheesecloth and discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with angina because of its unique composition, which includes sesquiterpenes that have a calming effect on the heart. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils' ability to dilate the coronary arteries allows for increased oxygen delivery to the heart muscle, alleviating chest pain associated with angina.

Cardiovascular-protective properties: The oil's cardio-protective compounds may help regulate blood flow, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiac function, alleviating angina episodes and reducing the risk of heart disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning at peak potency.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove impurities thoroughly.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a copper still and 10ml of pure water for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through a paper filter into a dark glass bottle immediately.
Store the filtered eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months without exposure.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves from a reputable source in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the fresh leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours.
Steam-distill the dried leaves using 10 grams of leaves per 100 milliliters of water for 30 minutes to obtain essential oil.
Filter the distilled mixture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store it away from direct sunlight for up to three months.
Mix one teaspoon of the prepared essential oil with one tablespoon of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for topical application three times daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark and clean it thoroughly with distilled water and mild soap solution.
Dry the cleaned bark in a shaded area with good air circulation for at least seven days.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark using a distillation apparatus with 1 liter of distilled water for two hours.
Collect the essential oil obtained through distillation and filter it using paper towels or cotton cloth to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dry place at a temperature below 60°f for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus x aurantium peel in late afternoon when oil content is highest for better yields.
Wash the peel thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then dry.
Steam distill the dried peel using a still or apparatus with a 10:1 ratio of water to peel for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol by allowing it to settle for 30 minutes then siphon off the clear oil layer.
Transfer the obtained citrus x aurantium essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with angina because of its natural ability to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure. remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves from a healthy plant in the morning after dew has evaporated, collecting 20 grams of fresh material.
Wash the collected thymus vulgaris leaves gently with cold distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with paper towels.
Steam distill the washed and dried thymus vulgaris leaves using a clevenger apparatus for 4-5 hours to extract essential oil, yielding 0.5 grams of clear liquid.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or residue, then store in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Allow the filtered thymus vulgaris essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, allowing its properties and aroma to fully develop and stabilize.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with angina because of its natural ability to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for anginal episodes. The oil's calming properties can help to alleviate feelings of tension and promote relaxation, allowing the body to respond more efficiently to physical exertion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in mid-summer morning when dew is still present on the plants for optimal oil yield and quality.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a copper alembic still at atmospheric pressure with 30 kilograms of fresh leaves per run for 3 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a glass wool or paper filter to remove impurities and sediment in approximately 1 hour.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles filled to the neck with an inert gas such as nitrogen or argon and seal tightly within 30 minutes of distillation completion.
Store the sealed bottles in a cool dark place at a temperature between 10°c to 15°c for at least 24 hours before using or selling to allow for stabilization of the oil properties.

Barks: Containing valuable flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular disorders, including angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Blend the leaves with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 10:1.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass bottle for 4-6 weeks at room temperature allowing infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and retaining essential oils.
Store the resulting ginkgo biloba essential oil in amber glass bottles away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's flavonoids and phenolic compounds help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms. remedy_properties: Sedative effect: The seed extract of P. incarnata may help reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.
Antispasmodic effect: The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids may help relax smooth muscle tissue, which could potentially relieve chest pain associated with angina.
Anti-inflammatory effect: The extract may possess anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are rich in saponins, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of angina by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in relieving angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers at dawn when dew is still present and gently rinse with filtered water.
Dry the plant material in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 7-10 days or until it reaches a moisture content of around 12%.
Combine 200 grams of dried passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers with 400 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 21 days, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction of essential oils from plant material.
Filter the resulting liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard solids after 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat angina with herbal essential oils is to use a blend of lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus oils. Add 2-3 drops of each oil to a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and massage it onto the chest and shoulders twice daily. This helps to reduce pain, improve circulation, and promote relaxation. Additionally, inhaling these oils through steam inhalation or using a diffuser can also provide relief from angina symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to angina that are treated with herbal essential oils are cardiovascular conditions such as high blood pressure, palpitations, and coronary heart disease. Essential oils like Lavender, Geranium, and Clary Sage have been used traditionally to help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving circulation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with angina because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation in the blood vessels. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to relax and dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased blood flow to the heart. This improved circulation helps to reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks, providing relief from the symptoms of reduced blood flow to the heart muscle.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with angina by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to the symptoms of angina.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolide B): Help with angina by improving blood flow and reducing platelet aggregation, which can help prevent blood clots from forming.
Bilobalide (a terpenoid): Help with angina by reducing oxidative stress and improving blood flow to the heart, which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate angina symptoms.
Seeds: Used for their ginkgotoxin and bilobalide content, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, potentially helping to manage angina.

(Optional) Buds: Used due to their high content of bilobalide and flavonoids, which may contribute to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that could help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are fully ripe and dry.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to release their essential oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and excess particles.
Store the ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils helps with angina because it contains compounds that improve circulation and reduce inflammation in blood vessels. The oils' ability to dilate coronary arteries and improve microcirculation helps to increase oxygen delivery to the heart muscle, reducing symptoms of angina. Additionally, Panax ginseng's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to angina. By reducing stress and improving blood flow, Panax ginseng essential oils may provide relief from angina symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, alleviating angina symptoms.
Panaxydol: This terpene has vasodilatory properties, which can help widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing angina symptoms and improving overall cardiovascular health.
Ginsenolic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-platelet aggregation properties, which can help prevent blood clots from forming and reduce the risk of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of ginsenosides, which are believed to help reduce blood pressure and improve blood flow, alleviating angina symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides, but they may still contribute to the overall therapeutic effects of Panax ginseng essential oils.
Barks: Some studies suggest that the barks may contain unique compounds that enhance the efficacy of ginsenosides in treating angina, although more research is needed.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of panax ginseng roots from matured plants cultivated in shaded areas for 4-6 years.
Steam distill the ginseng roots for 4-6 hours at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled steam that contains the essential oil and separate it from the water.
Use a separator or a decanter to separate the essential oil from the water within 30 minutes.
Bottle the extracted panax ginseng essential oil in dark-tinted glass bottles with airtight lids immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can help alleviate angina symptoms. Additionally, its vasodilatory effects allow for increased blood supply to the heart, reducing oxygen demand and alleviating chest pain. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing angina symptoms and promoting cardiovascular health. Regular use may lead to improved symptoms and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the severity of angina symptoms.
Shogaols: They exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and improve cardiovascular function, which helps alleviate angina pain and discomfort.
Zingiberene: It has vasodilatory effects, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make essential oils, although to a lesser extent than the rhyzome and root. They still contain some active compounds that can contribute to the oil's therapeutic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of zingiber officinale in the early morning for maximum oil content.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and impurities then dry them.
Steam distill 100g of the dried roots in 3l of distilled water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled water and separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with angina because of its vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom to ensure highest oil content and quality.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.
Analyze the essential oil using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry to ensure it meets the required standards and purity.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature leaves and twigs from the crataegus monogyna plant at dawn to capture optimal essential oil content.

Steam distill the dried plant material in a 1:5 ratio with distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separatory funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with angina because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress levels, which are common triggers for angina episodes. Barks: They contain a mixture of sesquiterpenes and valepotriates, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40g of valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.

Steam distill the dried roots for 2-3 hours to release essential oil from the plant material.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rauvolfia serpentina contain alstonine, a compound that helps to dilate blood vessels, potentially relieving angina symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Rauvolfia serpentina contain reserpine, a compound that can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Rauvolfia serpentina contain serpentine, a compound that may help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of rauvolfia serpentina roots and cut them into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Steam distill the rauvolfia serpentina root pieces for 4 hours at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron membrane filter to remove any impurities.
Store the filtered rauvolfia serpentina essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature of 10-20 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with angina because of its vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart. The oil's calming effects also reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of angina attacks. Additionally, lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to a reduction in angina symptoms. By promoting overall cardiovascular health, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may provide a natural and complementary approach to managing angina.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a vasodilator, helping to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, thus alleviating angina symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood flow, contributing to angina relief.
Limonene: Possesses antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, potentially contributing to reduced angina severity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are widely used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in angina patients.
Stems: Lavender stems contain essential oils that can help lower blood pressure and reduce stress, which can contribute to angina relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak oil production in mid-summer with scissors carefully.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using a distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Collect the extracted essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using a coffee filter paper.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol essential oils helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and beta-caryophyllene, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation in blood vessels and preventing oxidative stress. This can lead to improved blood flow, reduced blood pressure, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, Commiphora molmol oil may help to relax blood vessels and improve overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_constituents: β-Bisabolol: It helps with angina by acting as a vasodilator, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing the severity of angina symptoms.
β-Sitosterol: It helps with angina by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving lipid profiles, which reduces the risk of blood clots and atherosclerosis, contributing to angina development.

Commiphoric acid: It helps with angina by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, contributing to angina relief.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used for making essential oils due to their medicinal properties that help to treat various cardiovascular conditions, including angina.
Buds: Utilized in essential oil production for their therapeutic benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.
Stems: Employed in essential oil production to leverage their bioactive compounds that help in managing angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3 kg of dried commiphora molmol resin from the tree at the peak of resin production.
Steam distill the dried resin for 4 hours with 5 liters of water at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and let it settle for 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It contains cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce platelet aggregation, which can contribute to angina symptoms. Additionally, the oil's warming and stimulating properties may help to relax blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the heart, alleviating chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. Its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress may also contribute to its therapeutic benefits.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Helps with angina by exhibiting vasodilatory properties, which can increase blood flow to the heart and reduce blood pressure.
Eugenol: Contributes to reducing angina by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving blood circulation, thus reducing the risk of heart attack.
Linalool: May help alleviate angina symptoms by acting as an antioxidant and reducing oxidative stress in the blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum verum are commonly used to make essential oils, as they are rich in cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, potentially helping with angina symptoms.

Buds: Cinnamomum verum buds are often used in aromatherapy to create essential oils, as they contain compounds like linalool and eugenol, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with angina.

Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum verum are sometimes used to make essential oils, as they contain cinnamaldehyde and other compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially helping to reduce the severity of angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of fresh cinnamomum verum bark, selecting only the inner bark for distillation.
Steam distill the cinnamomum verum bark at 3.5 bar pressure for 4 hours to release oils.
Separate the essential oils from the water using a separating funnel after 2 hours of distillation.
Filter the essential oil using a 5-micron filter paper to remove impurities and debris.
Store the cinnamomum verum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
